name: research-driven-planning
description: Loop 1 of the Three-Loop Integrated Development System. Research-driven requirements analysis with iterative risk mitigation through 5x pre-mortem cycles using multi-agent consensus. Feeds validated, risk-mitigated plans to parallel-swarm-implementation. Use when starting new features or projects requiring comprehensive planning with <3% failure confidence and evidence-based technology selection.
qualityReason: Clear integration of research and planning
capabilities: Coordinates 6-agent parallel research with self-consistency validation
Generates evidence-based technology recommendations with confidence scores
Creates structured SPEC.md with complete requirements capture
Produces risk-mitigated plans with less than 3 percent failure confidence target
author: DNYoussef
securitySummary: Documentation-only skill containing no executable code. All 193 static findings are false positives triggered by documentation patterns: shell syntax examples, markdown code formatting, Graphviz diagram labels, and JSON metadata. The skill describes a project planning methodology with example commands and workflow diagrams.
valueStatement: Stop building on assumptions. This skill uses 6-agent research teams and 5-iteration pre-mortem analysis to create evidence-based project plans with less than 3 percent failure confidence.
userTitle: Plan Projects with Research-Based Risk Mitigation
limitations: Requires 6 to 11 hours for comprehensive planning phase
Needs Claude Code with Task tool support for multi-agent coordination
Depends on available research sources for evidence quality
Pre-mortem convergence may require up to 10 iterations for complex projects

useCases.title: Design complex distributed systems
useCases.description: Research authentication patterns, evaluate security frameworks, and pre-mortem scalability failures before building microservices.

useCases.title: Validate technical decisions
useCases.description: Evidence-based technology selection with risk analysis prevents costly pivots and ensures 2 to 3 times ROI through failure prevention.

useCases.title: Plan compliance-critical features
useCases.description: Comprehensive research on regulatory requirements and security standards with documented mitigation strategies for audit trails.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Project Planning
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to build a user authentication system with JWT tokens, refresh tokens, and role-based access control. The API uses Express.js and PostgreSQL. Use research-driven planning to create a comprehensive specification and risk-mitigated implementation plan.

promptTemplates.title: Complex System Architecture
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a real-time notification system that handles 1M concurrent WebSocket connections across multiple regions. Include message queuing, rate limiting, and failover strategies. Perform comprehensive research on proven patterns and pre-mortem all failure modes.

promptTemplates.title: Security-Critical Application
promptTemplates.prompt: Build a HIPAA-compliant patient data processing pipeline with end-to-end encryption, audit logging, and data anonymization. Research regulatory requirements and security best practices. Include Byzantine consensus pre-mortem for all compliance risks.

promptTemplates.title: Legacy System Migration
promptTemplates.prompt: Migrate a monolithic Java EE application to cloud-native microservices while maintaining zero downtime. Research strangler fig patterns, database migration strategies, and circuit breaker implementations. Plan for rollback scenarios and data consistency challenges.

bestPractices: Provide detailed constraints including timeline, budget, and technical limitations to focus research effectively
Review research synthesis for confidence scores below 70 percent and request additional evidence for critical decisions
Iterate pre-mortem cycles if failure confidence remains above 3 percent after 5 iterations
antiPatterns: Skipping research phase for familiar technologies. Hidden risks emerge in production.
Accepting low Byzantine consensus agreement below 66 percent. Indicates insufficient risk analysis.
Rushing through specification. Incomplete requirements cause 40 percent of project failures.

faq.question: Is this skill compatible with non-Claude Code environments?
faq.answer: No, this skill requires Claude Code Task tool for multi-agent coordination and memory system integration.

faq.question: What happens if pre-mortem does not converge to less than 3 percent confidence?
faq.answer: Skill runs up to 10 iterations. If still above 3 percent, provides recommendations to break down tasks further or add constraints.

faq.question: How does this integrate with existing project management tools?
faq.answer: Loop 1 outputs standard JSON planning packages and SPEC.md files that can be imported into Jira, Linear, or other tools.

faq.question: Is my project data safe during research?
faq.answer: Skill only uses public research sources and stores data locally. No proprietary information is sent to external services.

faq.question: The research phase is taking too long. How can I speed it up?
faq.answer: Use quick research depth option for faster results, or provide specific technology constraints to narrow research scope.

faq.question: How is this different from standard project planning?
faq.answer: Traditional planning relies on assumptions. This uses evidence-based research with 6-agent validation and Byzantine consensus for 85 to 95 percent failure prevention.
